# Lexicon Coverage Report (generated by eval/lexicon_coverage.py)

Pool size: **100000**.

## Primary STRONG-concept depth distribution
Distinct concepts in the primary evidence text (summary + role titles):

| distinct concepts | candidates | evidence grade |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 99821 | 0.5 (generic ML) / 0.6 (corroborated) / 0.0 |
| 1 | 35 | 0.70 |
| 2 | 86 | 0.85 |
| 3+ | 58 | 1.00 |

The lexicon credits builder-depth for **179** candidates (0.179% of the pool) — high precision, intentionally low recall.

## Off-lexicon builder-vocabulary recall test
34 builder synonyms NOT in the lexicon were scanned.

- Candidates using any in their **summary** (author-written): **252**
- Candidates using any only in **descriptions** (recycled boilerplate, non-distinctive): 325

### Off-lexicon terms that actually appear in summaries
| term | summary occurrences |
|---|---|
| collaborative filtering | 252 |

**33 of 34** tested synonyms have ZERO summary occurrences, including every system-noun the review names (discovery engine, search relevance, query understanding, two-tower, solr, …).

## Conclusion
The pool is templated, so candidate summaries draw from a fixed vocabulary. The hand lexicon was built against that vocabulary, so its narrow recall is near-fully-covering here: the only genuine off-lexicon builder term is `collaborative filtering`. Whether that one term should be credited as a recommendation concept is a scoring judgment evaluated separately (see eval/impact.py); it is not evidence of broad lexicon under-coverage.
